zh.lifeSummary: Mrs. James於大約1477年出生。她於大約1508年與James Brenchele在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs. James于大约1477年出生。她于大约1508年与James Brenchele在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: from the Middle English personal name James. Introduced to England by the Normans, this is an Old French form of Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Latin Iacobus, Greek Iakōbos, the New Testament rendering of Hebrew Ya‘aqob (see Jacob ). The medieval Latin (Vulgate) Bible distinguished between Old Testament Iacob (which was uninflected) and New Testament Iacobus (with inflections). The latter developed into James in medieval French. The distinction was carried over into the King James Bible of 1611, and Jacob and James remain as separate names in English usage. Most European languages, however, make no such distinction, so that forms such as French Jacques , stand for both the Old and the New Testament names. This surname is also very common among African Americans. Compare Jack .

variants.name: Giacomo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenico, Gino, Salvatore.
Italian: from the personal name Giacomo, from Latin Jacobus via Late Latin Jacomus (see James and Jacob ).

variants.name: Jamie
meanings: Scottish (mainly Angus): from a Scots pet form of the personal name   James  .

variants.name: Jemison
meanings: English (northern): patronymic from the personal name Jem (a pet form of James ) + son. Compare Jamison .

variants.name: Jemmett
meanings: English (southern): from the Middle English personal name Jemet, a diminutive of Jem, a pet form of James .

variants.name: Jaime
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jesus, Jorge, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Javier, Arturo, Luis, Alfredo, Enrique, Miguel.
Spanish: from the personal name Jaime, an equivalent of James .

variants.name: Jamison
meanings: English and Scottish: from Middle English and Scots Jami(e)son, Jameson, Jem(i)son ‘son of James’ (see James ). Jameson and Jamison are mainly found in northern England, especially in Northumberland and Durham, while in Scotland the more usual form is Jamieson , but all three versions occur on either side of the border.

variants.name: Jacobo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jesus, Francisco, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Rafael, Alfredo, Luis, Carlos, Mario, Roberto.
Spanish and Italian: from the Latin personal name Jacobus (see James , Giacomo ).

variants.name: Jemmott
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alicia, Armando, Dionicio, Gregoria, Guillermo, Roberto, Rosita, Ruperto.
English: from the personal name Jemot, a diminutive of Jem, a pet form of James . This surname is frequent in Barbados, Trinidad and Tobago, and Saint Lucia.

variants.name: Como
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Salvatore, Giuseppe, Ignazio, Nunzio, Vito, Angelo, Antonio, Camillo, Carlo, Carmela, Caterina.
Italian: from the personal name Como, a short form of Giacomo, an equivalent of James .
Italian: habitational name from Como in Lombardy.

variants.name: Jacob
meanings: Jewish, English, Welsh, German, Portuguese, French (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, Swedish, Danish, and Norwegian: derivative, via Latin Jacob(us), from the Hebrew personal name Ya‘aqob (Yaakov). In the Bible, this is the name of the crafty younger twin brother of Esau (Genesis 25:26), who took advantage of the latter's hunger and impetuousness to persuade him to part with his birthright ‘for a mess of potage’. The name is traditionally interpreted as coming from Hebrew akev ‘heel’: Jacob is said to have been born holding on to Esau's heel. In English usage the name Jacob is regarded as distinct from the name James , but they are of identical origin. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Assyrian/Chaldean or Arabic Yaqub , Yakub , Yacoub , or Yacob , Slovenian Jakob and Jakop, Czech and Slovak Jakub , and also their patronymics and other derivatives (see examples at Jacobs and Jacobson ). The name Jacob is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Chacko ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. James was born about 1477. She married James Brenchele about 1508, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs. James est née environ 1477. Elle a épousé James Brenchele environ 1508, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs. James wurde um 1477 geboren. Sie hat um 1508 in England, United Kingdom James Brenchele geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs. James è nata il circa 1477. Lei sposato James Brenchele circa 1508, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Mrs. Jamesは約1477年に生まれた。約1508年にイギリスイギリスでJames Brencheleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. James(은)는 대략 1477년 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1508년 영국 잉글랜드에서 James Brenchele(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs. James nasceu aproximadamente 1477. Ela casou-se com James Brenchele aproximadamente 1508, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs. James родилась около 1477 г. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени James Brenchele около 1508 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs. James nació aproximadamente 1477. Se casó con James Brenchele aproximadamente 1508, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
